attractions.name: Beauregard Town Historic District
attractions.description: Beauregard Town is the second-oldest neighborhood in Baton Rouge, commissioned in 1806 with a grandiose Baroque "Grand Manner" city plan. This National Historic District features a unique configuration of diagonal streets crossing a grid pattern and is renowned for its well-preserved 19th and early 20th-century architecture, including Queen Anne and Colonial Revival styles. Wikipedia, Downtown Development District
attractions.address: Roughly bounded by North Blvd., South Blvd., East Blvd., and St. Louis St.
attractions.hours: Open 24/7 (Public neighborhood); daytime recommended for architectural tours. Airial Travel
attractions.admission: Free Airial Travel
highlights: Unique Baroque street layout with four diagonal avenues radiating from a central point. 64 Parishes
Over 450 historic structures showcasing Queen Anne, Colonial Revival, and Craftsman architecture. National Register of Historic Places
Home to significant landmarks like the Old Louisiana Governor's Mansion. Wikipedia
Picturesque tree-lined streets named after European royalty and world continents. Natasha Engle
Integration with the historic "Catfish Town" warehouse district near the Mississippi River. Yahoo News
attractions.tips: The neighborhood is best explored on foot due to its high walkability and concentration of historic homes. Visitors should be mindful of limited street parking during business hours when many residences serve as law offices. Airial Travel
attractions.nearby: Old State Capitol, Louisiana State Capitol, USS Kidd Veterans Museum, Louisiana Art & Science Museum, Belle of Baton Rouge Casino. Expedia

attractions.name: Firehouse Gallery (Arts Council of Greater Baton Rouge)
attractions.description: The Firehouse Gallery, operated by the Arts Council of Greater Baton Rouge, is a dynamic exhibition space dedicated to promoting local and regional artists through ever-evolving contemporary and traditional art shows. Now located within the 12,000-square-foot Cary Saurage Community Arts Center, the gallery serves as a vital platform for creative expression and community engagement in the heart of downtown Baton Rouge. Arts Council of Greater Baton Rouge, 225 Magazine
attractions.hours: Monday - Thursday: 8:30 AM - 4:30 PM; Friday: 8:30 AM - 3:30 PM; Saturday: 10:00 AM - 2:00 PM; Sunday: Closed Arts Council of Greater Baton Rouge
attractions.admission: Free Arts Council of Greater Baton Rouge
attractions.phone: 225-344-8558 Arts Council of Greater Baton Rouge
highlights: Rotating exhibitions featuring visual artists from the 11-parish Greater Baton Rouge region.
Monthly 'First Wednesday' opening receptions with live music and refreshments.
Access to the Arts Council’s permanent collection, featuring paintings, stained glass, and sculpture.
Located within a state-of-the-art facility including a black box theater and recording studio.
Integration with the Baton Rouge Arts Market for direct artist-to-public sales.
Educational workshops and artist talks held regularly in the community spaces.
attractions.tips: Visit during the 'First Wednesday' receptions to meet local artists and enjoy a lively social atmosphere. Check the online calendar for special performances or workshops in the adjoining Black Box Studio or on the rooftop terrace. Arts Council of Greater Baton Rouge, 225 Magazine
attractions.nearby: Old State Capitol, Shaw Center for the Arts, Louisiana Art & Science Museum, USS Kidd Veterans Museum, Riverfront Plaza Visit Baton Rouge

attractions.name: Louisiana Art & Science Museum (LASM)
attractions.description: Housed in a historic 1925 railroad depot on the banks of the Mississippi River, the Louisiana Art & Science Museum (LASM) offers a unique fusion of art and science exhibitions. The museum features internationally renowned art, an Ancient Egypt gallery, and the state-of-the-art Irene W. Pennington Planetarium, providing educational and entertaining experiences for visitors of all ages.
attractions.hours: Wednesday-Friday: 10:00 AM - 3:00 PM; Saturday: 10:00 AM - 5:00 PM; Sunday: 1:00 PM - 5:00 PM; Monday & Tuesday: Closed (except for reserved school groups on Tuesdays).
attractions.admission: Adult: $16.00, Child (3-12): $13.00, Senior (65+): $13.00; Free for active-duty military, veterans, first responders, and their families; Free admission every first Sunday of the month (planetarium shows $5).
highlights: Ancient Egypt Gallery featuring a 2,300-year-old Ptolemaic mummy
Irene W. Pennington Planetarium with surround-sound fulldome shows
Authentic 65-million-year-old Triceratops skull named "Jason"
Solar System and Universe Galleries with interactive astronomical displays
Hands-on art and science stations for children and families
Historic Illinois Central Railroad Passenger Station building architecture
attractions.tips: Parking in downtown Baton Rouge is free on weekends and after 6:00 PM on weekdays. Take advantage of the First Free Sunday program for complimentary museum entry, and remember that general admission includes unlimited planetarium shows. The last entry for the day is at 4:00 PM on Saturdays and Sundays.
attractions.nearby: Old State Capitol, Old Governor's Mansion, USS Kidd Veterans Museum, Shaw Center for the Arts, Mississippi River Riverfront.

attractions.name: Louisiana's Old State Capitol (Official Website)
attractions.description: Constructed between 1847 and 1852, this Gothic Revival landmark features a medieval fortress-like exterior and a stunning interior with a stained-glass dome and a spiral cast-iron staircase (Official Website). It serves as a Museum of Political History, interpreting Louisiana's flamboyant past through interactive exhibits and an award-winning multimedia presentation (Wikipedia, Visit Baton Rouge).
attractions.hours: Tuesday-Friday: 10:00 AM – 4:00 PM; Saturday: 9:00 AM – 3:00 PM; Sunday-Monday: Closed (Official Website)
attractions.days_open: Tuesday, Wednesday, Thursday, Friday, Saturday (Official Website)
attractions.admission: Admission to the museum and self-guided tours is Free (Official Website, Visit Baton Rouge); \"The Ghost of the Castle\" show is $2 per person (Official Website).
highlights: Gothic Revival architecture often called the \\"Castle on the River\\" (Visit Baton Rouge)
A magnificent stained-glass cathedral dome and a spiral cast-iron staircase (Official Website)
\\"The Ghost of the Castle\\" 4D immersive theatrical presentation (Official Website)
Extensive exhibits on Louisiana's political history, including Governor Huey Long (Official Website)
Stunning views of the Mississippi River from its bluff-top location (Visit Baton Rouge)
State-of-the-art interactive technology throughout the museum galleries (Official Website)
attractions.tips: Meter parking is available on the sides of the building, and a parking garage is located nearby (Official Website FAQ). No food or drinks are allowed inside, and photography sessions require advance appointments and fees for indoor use (Official Website FAQ). A handicap-accessible entrance is available at the back of the building (Official Website FAQ).
attractions.nearby: Current Louisiana State Capitol, USS Kidd Veterans Museum, Louisiana Art & Science Museum, Shaw Center for the Arts, and Capitol Park Museum (Visit Baton Rouge, Lonely Planet).

attractions.name: LSU Museum of Art
attractions.description: The LSU Museum of Art, located within the Shaw Center for the Arts, serves as a premier cultural destination featuring over 6,500 works of art across 14 galleries. It houses one of the most comprehensive collections of Louisiana art and a world-class collection of Chinese jade, while also hosting diverse touring exhibitions from around the globe (LSU Museum of Art, Visit Baton Rouge).
attractions.address: 100 Lafayette Street, Fifth Floor, Shaw Center for the Arts
attractions.hours: Monday: Closed; Tuesday: 10 AM – 5 PM; Wednesday: 10 AM – 5 PM; Thursday: 10 AM – 5 PM; Friday: 10 AM – 5 PM; Saturday: 10 AM – 5 PM; Sunday: 1 PM – 5 PM (LSU Museum of Art)
attractions.admission: Adults (13+): $5; Children (12 and under): Free; LSU Students, Faculty, and Staff (with ID): Free; Educators: Free; Veterans and their families: Free; Museum Members: Free; First Sunday of every month: Free; Museums for All (with EBT card): Free for up to 4 individuals (LSU Museum of Art, Visit Baton Rouge)
highlights: A world-class collection of Chinese jade carvings (LSU Museum of Art)
One of the most comprehensive public collections of Louisiana art in the region (LSU Museum of Art)
Extensive permanent collection of over 6,500 objects, including works by Clementine Hunter and Walter Anderson (LSU Museum of Art)
Rotating touring exhibitions of regional, American, and European paintings and sculpture (LSU Museum of Art)
Significant collection of works on paper and photography, featuring artists like Rembrandt and Goya (LSU Museum of Art, Reddit)
Unique location on the 5th floor of the award-winning Shaw Center for the Arts building (Visit Baton Rouge)
attractions.tips: Take advantage of free admission on the first Sunday of every month. The museum store on the first floor of the Shaw Center offers unique local crafts and gifts. Non-flash photography is permitted for personal use in most galleries (LSU Museum of Art, LSU Museum of Art).
attractions.nearby: Old State Capitol, Louisiana Art & Science Museum (LASM), Manship Theatre, Mississippi River Levee Path, Old Governor's Mansion, Capitol Park Museum (Visit Baton Rouge, St James Place)

attractions.name: Mississippi Riverfront Baton Rouge (Riverfront Plaza & City Dock)
attractions.description: The Mississippi Riverfront in Baton Rouge is a scenic educational greenspace and outdoor event venue situated along the levee of one of North America's most significant waterways. It features a diverse landscape of water gardens, meadows, and walking/biking paths, offering panoramic views of the river and the iconic Horace Wilkinson Bridge (Visit Baton Rouge, Downtown Development District).
attractions.address: 300 River Road South, Baton Rouge, LA 70802-6219
attractions.hours: Monday: Open 24 hours, Tuesday: Open 24 hours, Wednesday: Open 24 hours, Thursday: Open 24 hours, Friday: Open 24 hours, Saturday: Open 24 hours, Sunday: Open 24 hours (Tripadvisor).
attractions.admission: Free for general park access; shoreside museum at nearby USS Kidd is $8 while ship is away for repairs (Finding the Universe).
highlights: Sing the River Sculpture: A reflective, musical sculpture that interacts with the river's water level (Visit Baton Rouge).
Riverfront Plaza & City Dock: A 5.4-acre space featuring an observation deck and access to the USS Kidd (Visit Baton Rouge).
Baton Rouge Levee Bike & Pedestrian Path: A paved trail popular for walking, jogging, and cycling (Downtown Development District).
Sunset Views: One of the city's premier spots for viewing and photographing sunsets over the Mississippi River (Finding the Universe).
Levee Stage: An outdoor performance area for concerts and events with the river as a backdrop (Visit Baton Rouge).
attractions.tips: Visit at dusk for the best photography of the Horace Wilkinson Bridge lights; arrive 30-45 minutes before sunset for optimal lighting (Finding the Universe). As of May 2026, the USS Kidd ship is temporarily away for repairs, but the shoreside museum remains open (Finding the Universe).
attractions.nearby: USS Kidd Veterans Museum, Louisiana Art & Science Museum, Raising Cane's River Center, Shaw Center for the Arts, Louisiana's Old State Capitol (Downtown Development District).

attractions.name: Oxbow Rum Distillery (formerly Cane Land Distilling Company)
attractions.description: Oxbow Rum Distillery, formerly known as Cane Land Distilling Company, is a family-owned, "cane-to-glass" operation that produces premium estate rums using sugarcane grown on their 5th-generation family farm. Located in downtown Baton Rouge, the distillery specializes in small-batch, additive-free spirits distilled in a copper hybrid pot still to highlight the natural flavors of Louisiana sugarcane.
attractions.address: 760 St Philip St, Baton Rouge, LA 70802
attractions.hours: Monday: Closed; Tuesday: Closed; Wednesday: Closed; Thursday: 12:00 PM – 8:00 PM; Friday: 12:00 PM – 8:00 PM; Saturday: 12:00 PM – 8:00 PM; Sunday: 12:00 PM – 8:00 PM
attractions.admission: $15 per person for Distillery Tour & Tasting (reservations recommended). Free entry to the Tasting Room and outdoor patio for cocktails and spirit purchases.
highlights: Single estate rum made from 100% Grade A sugarcane molasses grown on the family's farm since 1859.
Award-winning spirits including the 'Best in Class' Oxbow Small Batch White Rum.
On-site Tasting Room offering signature cocktails like the Oxbow Mojito and False River Canekiller.
Small-batch production utilizing a traditional copper hybrid pot still for superior flavor profiles.
Pet and family-friendly outdoor patio featuring artifacts from the family's sugar mill.
Ranked No. 6 Best Craft Rum by USA Today 10Best Readers' Choice Awards.
attractions.tips: Visitors should arrive at least 15 minutes early for scheduled tours and must present a valid ID for tastings. While walk-ins are welcome in the tasting room, distillery tours should be booked in advance via their website to ensure availability.
attractions.nearby: The 13th Gate Haunted House, Louisiana's Old State Capitol, LSU Museum of Art, Shaw Center for the Arts, Mississippi Riverfront.

attractions.name: Raising Cane's River Center Arena
attractions.description: The Raising Cane’s River Center Arena is a premier 10,000-seat multi-purpose venue located in the heart of downtown Baton Rouge, Louisiana. Originally opened in 1977, it serves as a central hub for concerts, sporting events, and family entertainment, and can be combined with the adjacent exhibition hall for large-scale conventions. The arena is home to local sports teams and cultural organizations, playing a key role in the city's entertainment landscape. Source: Wikipedia, Official Website
attractions.hours: Monday: 10:00 AM - 4:00 PM, Tuesday: 10:00 AM - 4:00 PM, Wednesday: 10:00 AM - 4:00 PM, Thursday: 10:00 AM - 4:00 PM, Friday: 10:00 AM - 4:00 PM, Saturday: Closed (unless event), Sunday: Closed (unless event). Source: Official Website
attractions.admission: Varies by event; typically available via Ticketmaster and AXS. Children under two years of age often do not require a ticket for most events. Source: Official Website
highlights: 10,000-seat capacity for concerts and sporting events. Source: Wikipedia
Home to the Baton Rouge Symphony Orchestra and Baton Rouge Ballet. Source: Official Website
Part of a larger complex with over 200,000 square feet of convention and exhibit space. Source: Official Website
Cashless venue policy for all concessions and parking transactions. Source: Official Website
Strict clear bag policy for enhanced security during all events. Source: Official Website
Hosted professional teams like the Baton Rouge Zydeco and Baton Rouge Kingfish. Source: Wikipedia
attractions.tips: Visitors are encouraged to purchase parking in advance. The arena operates as a cashless venue, accepting only digital payments for concessions. Ensure compliance with the clear bag policy to avoid entry delays. Door times vary but typically open one hour before the scheduled start time. Source: Official Website
attractions.nearby: USS Kidd, Louisiana's Old State Capitol, Riverfront Plaza, Louisiana Art & Science Museum, Repentance Park. Source: Tripadvisor

attractions.name: Shaw Center for the Arts
attractions.description: The Shaw Center for the Arts is a 125,000-square-foot performing arts venue, fine arts museum, and education center that covers an entire city block in downtown Baton Rouge. It serves as a major cultural hub, featuring a variety of galleries, a theater, and several dining options with spectacular views of the Mississippi River. The building itself is an award-winning architectural landmark known for its translucent glass facade (Official Website, Wikipedia).
attractions.hours: Monday: 9:00 AM – 4:00 PM; Tuesday–Thursday: 9:00 AM – 10:00 PM; Friday: 9:00 AM – 11:00 PM; Saturday: 10:00 AM – 11:00 PM; Sunday: 11:00 AM – 5:00 PM (Note: Individual venue and restaurant hours may vary) (Official Website)
attractions.admission: Building entry: Free; LSU Museum of Art: $5 for adults (13+), Free for children (12 & under), students, and veterans; Manship Theatre: Prices vary by event (LSU Museum of Art, Vivid Seats)
highlights: LSU Museum of Art featuring a diverse permanent collection and rotating exhibitions
Manship Theatre, a 325-seat premier performing arts venue
Tsunami, a rooftop sushi restaurant offering panoramic views of the Mississippi River
LSU School of Art Glassell Gallery showcasing contemporary works
The River Terrace, a stunning outdoor event space overlooking the water
Interactive public fountain located at the center's entrance (Official Website, Tripadvisor)
attractions.tips: Head to the rooftop for a drink or meal at Tsunami to enjoy the best sunset views in the city. The LSU Museum of Art offers free admission on the first Sunday of every month and every Thursday evening from 5:00 PM to 8:00 PM. If street parking is unavailable, use the public parking garage located at the corner of Third and Convention Streets (Official Website, LSU Museum of Art).
attractions.nearby: Louisiana's Old State Capitol, USS Kidd Veterans Museum, Louisiana Art & Science Museum, Riverfront Plaza, Louisiana State Capitol (Tripadvisor)

attractions.name: Spanish Town Historic District
attractions.description: Founded in 1805, Spanish Town is Baton Rouge's oldest neighborhood and a vibrant historic district known for its diverse architectural history. It is world-renowned for its irreverent Mardi Gras parade and the iconic pink flamingos that serve as its mascot. Spanish Town Website, Wikipedia
attractions.address: Bounded by State Capitol Drive, North 5th Street, North 9th Street, and North Street
attractions.hours: Open 24 hours (Neighborhood) BRLA.gov
attractions.admission: Free (Neighborhood/Public District) Facebook
highlights: Oldest neighborhood in Baton Rouge, established in 1805 Official Website
Home to the city's largest and most famous Mardi Gras parade Wikipedia
Iconic Pink Flamingo mascot and traditions Wikipedia
Rich architectural diversity featuring bungalows and Victorian cottages Wikipedia
Listed on the National Register of Historic Places Wikipedia
Located in the shadow of the Louisiana State Capitol Official Website
attractions.tips: Visit during the Saturday before Mardi Gras for the famous parade, but arrive early as parking is limited. The neighborhood is best explored on foot to appreciate the historic homes and the local 'Pink Flamingo' decor. Reddit, Visit Baton Rouge
attractions.nearby: Louisiana State Capitol, Capitol Park Museum, Old State Capitol, USS Kidd Veterans Museum Finding the Universe

attractions.name: Baton Rouge Gallery - center for contemporary art
attractions.description: Baton Rouge Gallery is a non-profit contemporary art gallery founded in 1966, making it one of the oldest professional artist co-ops in the nation. It showcases a rotating selection of contemporary works from more than 60 professional artist members across various media including painting, sculpture, and photography.
attractions.hours: Monday: Closed; Tuesday - Sunday: 12:00 PM - 6:00 PM (Baton Rouge Gallery)
attractions.admission: Free (Baton Rouge Gallery)
highlights: Rotating monthly exhibitions featuring local and national contemporary artists.
Movies & Music on the Lawn program featuring silent films with live original scores.
First Wednesday Opening Receptions with complimentary refreshments and live music.
ARTiculate Artist Talk series providing insight into the creative processes of featured artists.
Housed in the historic City Park Pavilion, which originally served as a pool house in the 1920s.
BRG@BTR program that displays local art at the Baton Rouge Metropolitan Airport.
attractions.tips: Visit on the first Wednesday of each month for exhibition opening receptions featuring live music and refreshments. Check the schedule for 'Movies & Music on the Lawn' to catch unique silent film screenings with live scores. Since the gallery is located in City Park, visitors can also enjoy the surrounding park grounds and nearby attractions after their visit. Admission is always free, though donations to the non-profit are welcome.
attractions.nearby: Knock Knock Children's Museum, LSU Campus, Magnolia Mound Plantation, Louisiana Art & Science Museum, Shaw Center for the Arts (Visit Baton Rouge)

attractions.name: Brew Ha-Ha! (Official Website)
attractions.category: Coffee Shop, Cafe, Bakery, and Art Gallery
attractions.description: Brew Ha-Ha! is a vibrant, locally-owned coffee shop and bakery that has been a staple of the Mid City community since 2004. Known as the 'Original Baton Rouge Ballers,' they are famous for their unique cake balls available in over 100 flavors, alongside house-roasted coffee and a menu that includes smoothies and light fare. The space also serves as a welcoming community hub, doubling as an art gallery for local creators. (Official Website, Visit Baton Rouge)
attractions.address: 711 Jefferson Hwy. Ste. 2-A, Baton Rouge, LA 70806 (Official Website)
attractions.hours: Monday - Thursday: 7:00 AM - 7:00 PM; Friday: 7:00 AM - 5:00 PM; Saturday - Sunday: 8:00 AM - 4:00 PM (Official Website)
attractions.admission: Free entry; items available for purchase (Official Website)
highlights: Famous 'Original Baton Rouge Ballers' cake balls in 100+ flavors (Visit Baton Rouge)
House-roasted coffee from BHH Roast & Co. (Official Website)
Community art gallery space featuring local artists (Official Website)
Expanded menu including protein-packed smoothies and healthy options (225 Magazine)
Vibrant atmosphere ideal for study sessions, meetings, or first dates (Tripadvisor)
attractions.tips: The shop is located in a strip mall and can be easy to miss from the street; it's a popular spot for groups, so expect a lively atmosphere rather than complete quiet. Be sure to try their signature cake balls, which are the main attraction. (Tripadvisor, Yelp)
attractions.nearby: The Bougie Bar, Goodwood Park, Elizabethan Gallery, and various Mid City restaurants (Tripadvisor, Visit Baton Rouge)

attractions.name: Independence Community Park BREC
attractions.description: Independence Community Park is a sprawling 96-acre oasis managed by BREC in the heart of Baton Rouge. It serves as a vibrant community hub featuring award-winning botanical gardens, diverse sports facilities, and a professional theatre, designed to provide a full day of recreation for visitors of all ages. Visit Baton Rouge, BREC
attractions.address: 7500 Independence Boulevard Visit Baton Rouge, BREC
attractions.hours: Daily, 7:00 AM – 7:00 PM (Botanical Gardens); Park generally open sunrise to sunset. Whichmuseum, Visit Baton Rouge
attractions.admission: Free for general park and botanical garden access; fees apply for Liberty Lagoon waterpark, Independence Park Theatre events, and facility rentals. BREC, Airial Travel
highlights: Independence Botanical Gardens (Rose, Sensory, and Crape Myrtle gardens) BREC
Liberty Lagoon Waterpark BREC
Independence Park Theatre & Cultural Center theparktheatre.com
Tennis Center with indoor and outdoor courts Visit Baton Rouge
"Play for All" Inclusive Playground Visit Baton Rouge
1.8-mile walking trail system BREC
attractions.tips: Visit the botanical gardens in spring mornings for peak blooms. The park is adjacent to the Main Library at Goodwood, which includes a garden cafe. Comfortable walking shoes are recommended for the nearly 2-mile walking trail. Airial Travel, BREC
attractions.nearby: East Baton Rouge Parish Main Library (Goodwood), Mid City neighborhood, Independence Event Center. BREC, Finding the Universe

attractions.name: Mid City Baton Rouge (Mid City Cultural District)
attractions.description: Mid City Baton Rouge is a vibrant, eclectic neighborhood known for its historic, oak-lined streets and thriving arts scene. It serves as the creative heart of the city, featuring a diverse mix of local boutiques, vintage shops, and iconic independent restaurants nestled between downtown and the Jefferson Highway area. Mid City Merchants, Visit Baton Rouge
attractions.address: Government St (from 14th St to Jefferson Hwy), Baton Rouge, LA 70806
attractions.region: Mid City / East Baton Rouge Parish
attractions.hours: Varies by business; common boutique hours are Mon-Sat 10:00 AM - 5:00 PM; many restaurants and bars open daily until late. Sweet Baton Rouge, Yelp

attractions.name: The Radio Bar (Official Website)
attractions.description: The Radio Bar is a trendy, mid-century modern-inspired venue known for its hipster-friendly atmosphere and vibrant nightlife in Baton Rouge's Mid City. It features a spacious patio where patrons can enjoy games like ping-pong and darts, along with a unique app-based jukebox system that allows guests to vote on the music being played. The bar offers a wide selection of craft cocktails and beer, making it a popular neighborhood gathering spot for groups and dates alike (The Gregory, Visit Baton Rouge).
attractions.address: 3079 Government Street, Ste. B, Baton Rouge, Louisiana 70806 (Official Website)
attractions.hours: Monday: 4:00 PM - 2:00 AM; Tuesday: 4:00 PM - 2:00 AM; Wednesday: 4:00 PM - 2:00 AM; Thursday: 4:00 PM - 2:00 AM; Friday: 3:00 PM - 2:00 AM; Saturday: 3:00 PM - 2:00 AM; Sunday: 3:00 PM - 12:00 AM (Tripadvisor, Evendo)

Interactive jukebox app for crowd-sourced music selection (The Gregory)
Extensive craft cocktail and beer menu (Evendo)
Dog-friendly environment (Visit Baton Rouge)
Daily happy hour specials until 7 PM (Facebook)
Modern, mid-century industrial decor (The Gregory)

Take advantage of the daily happy hour specials until 7 PM, and don't forget to download the music app to contribute to the bar's playlist. The patio is a great spot if you're bringing your dog (Tripadvisor, Visit Baton Rouge).

attractions.name: Alex Box Stadium, Skip Bertman Field
attractions.description: Alex Box Stadium, Skip Bertman Field is a premier collegiate baseball stadium and home to the multi-national champion LSU Tigers. Opened in 2009, the facility features a seating capacity of over 10,700 and is renowned for its electric atmosphere and record-breaking attendance figures. The stadium serves as a central hub for the LSU baseball legacy, hosting numerous NCAA regionals and super regionals. (LSU Sports, Wikipedia)
attractions.hours: Gamedays: Gates and Stadium Ticket Office open 2 hours prior to first pitch; Parking: Lots open 3 hours prior to each game; Main Ticket Office: Mon-Fri 9:00 AM - 5:00 PM (LSU Sports, LSU Sports)
attractions.days_open: Varies by game schedule; Ticket Office open Monday-Friday (LSU Sports)
attractions.admission: LSU Students: Free (regular season with valid ID); Single Game (2026): $4 - $30 depending on seat location and opponent; Season Tickets: $300 - $600 (plus Tradition Fund contributions) (LSU Sports)
highlights: Home to the LSU Tigers baseball team, one of the most successful programs in college baseball history (LSU Sports)
Consistently ranks No. 1 in the nation for total and average attendance (Wikipedia)
Named the 2009 American Sports Builders Association Facility of the Year (Wikipedia)
Features the 'Intimidator' billboard in right field displaying LSU's national championships (Wikipedia)
Includes the Wally Pontiff Jr. Baseball Hall of Fame on the ground level (LSU Sports)
State-of-the-art Marucci Performance Center for player training (Wikipedia)
attractions.tips: Adheres to the LSU 'Geaux Safe' clear bag policy; backpacks and coolers are prohibited. Fans may bring one factory-sealed water bottle (up to 34 oz). It is recommended to purchase tickets in advance via LSUtix.net due to high demand. (LSU Sports)
attractions.nearby: Tiger Stadium (LSU Football), Mike the Tiger Habitat, LSU Museum of Natural Science, Magnolia Mound Plantation, Louisiana State Capitol (Visit Baton Rouge)

attractions.name: Mike Anderson’s Seafood Restaurant and Oyster Bar
attractions.description: Established in 1975 by former LSU All-American linebacker Mike Anderson, this iconic South Louisiana restaurant is a staple for both food lovers and football fans. It offers a festive atmosphere and a rich culinary tradition focused on fresh Louisiana seafood, serving as a popular gathering spot for over four decades (Official Website).
attractions.hours: Sunday: 11:00 AM – 9:00 PM; Monday – Thursday: 11:00 AM – 2:00 PM & 5:00 PM – 9:00 PM; Friday – Saturday: 11:00 AM – 10:00 PM (Official Website)
attractions.days_open: Monday, Tuesday, Wednesday, Thursday, Friday, Saturday, Sunday (Official Website)
attractions.admission: Free (Official Website)
highlights: World-famous Chargrilled Oysters topped with garlic, butter, parmesan, and romano cheese (Visit Baton Rouge)
Signature dishes like the Guitreau (fish topped with crawfish and shrimp) and Shrimp Norman (Official Website)
Founded and owned by LSU football legend Mike Anderson, featuring a sports-themed atmosphere (Official Website)
Extensive menu featuring fresh Gulf catches, fried seafood platters, and authentic Cajun specialties (Yelp)
Award-winning bread pudding with signature rum sauce for dessert (Visit Baton Rouge)
attractions.tips: Due to its proximity to LSU, the restaurant is extremely busy on game days; calling for reservations is highly recommended (Official Website). Be mindful of the weekday split hours, as the restaurant closes between lunch and dinner (Tripadvisor).
attractions.nearby: LSU Tiger Stadium, Mike the Tiger's Habitat, Alex Box Stadium, and the Louisiana State University campus (Tripadvisor)

attractions.name: Mike the Tiger's Habitat
attractions.description: Mike the Tiger's Habitat is a 15,000-square-foot state-of-the-art facility that serves as the home for Louisiana State University's live Bengal tiger mascot. The environment features a natural terrain with lush plantings, a live oak tree, a waterfall, and a stream that empties into a wading pond. It is one of the largest and most advanced tiger preserves in the country and is fully licensed by the USDA (LSU Mike the Tiger, Wikipedia).
attractions.hours: Mike is outside daily between 8:00 AM and 7:00 PM, though the habitat grounds are accessible 24/7. Yard work occurs on Thursday mornings during which Mike remains inside (LSU Mike the Tiger, Tripadvisor).
attractions.days_open: Monday through Sunday (LSU Mike the Tiger)
attractions.admission: Free (LSU Mike the Tiger)
attractions.phone: 225-578-9922 (LSU School of Veterinary Medicine) (LSU Mike the Tiger)
highlights: Home to Mike VII, the only live tiger mascot living on a college campus in the U.S. (LSU Mike the Tiger)
State-of-the-art 15,000-square-foot enclosure with natural substrates and Italianate architectural elements (LSU Mike the Tiger)
Features a waterfall, wading pool, and a large Live Oak tree for the tiger's enrichment (LSU Mike the Tiger)
Fully funded by private donations through the Tiger Athletic Foundation (Wikipedia)
Accessible 24/7 to the public for viewing through the habitat's glass and fencing (Tripadvisor)
Equipped with a live "Tiger Cam" for remote viewing (LSU Mike the Tiger)
attractions.tips: Visit during the cooler parts of the day (morning or late afternoon) to see Mike most active. Check Mike's social media platforms for updates if he is being kept inside for maintenance or weather (LSU Mike the Tiger, Tripadvisor).
attractions.nearby: LSU Tiger Stadium, Pete Maravich Assembly Center, LSU SportShop, Memorial Tower, and the LSU Dairy Store (Explore Louisiana)

attractions.name: Pete Maravich Assembly Center
attractions.description: The Pete Maravich Assembly Center is a 13,215-seat multi-purpose arena that serves as the home court for LSU Tigers basketball, gymnastics, and volleyball. Opened in 1971 and renamed in 1988 to honor LSU legend "Pistol Pete" Maravich, the facility is famously known as the "Deaf Dome" for its incredibly loud and energetic atmosphere (LSU Sports, Visit Baton Rouge).
attractions.address: 1 N Stadium Rd, Baton Rouge, LA 70803
attractions.hours: Monday: 9:00 AM – 6:00 PM, Tuesday: 9:00 AM – 6:00 PM, Wednesday: 9:00 AM – 6:00 PM, Thursday: 9:00 AM – 6:00 PM, Friday: 9:00 AM – 6:00 PM, Saturday: Closed (Open for events), Sunday: Closed (Open for events) (Birdeye). Doors typically open 60-90 minutes before tipoff (SeatGeek).
attractions.days_open: Monday - Friday (General operations); Open on weekends for scheduled events (Birdeye).
attractions.admission: Varies by event; Average historical price around $42.49, with some events starting as low as $9 (Vivid Seats, SeatGeek).
highlights: Official home of LSU men's and women's basketball, gymnastics, and volleyball (LSU Sports)
Earned the nickname "Deaf Dome" due to its legendary acoustics and crowd noise (Visit Baton Rouge)
Features a bronze statue of "Pistol Pete" Maravich unveiled outside the arena in 2022 (Tiger Rag)
Concourse divided into four thematic quadrants: Pete Maravich Pass, The Walk of Champions, Heroes Hall, and Midway of Memories (Wikipedia)
State-of-the-art practice facility including duplicate full-size gymnasiums for men's and women's teams (LSU Sports)
attractions.tips: Strict clear bag policy is enforced (max 12” x 6” x 12”); arrive at least 90 minutes early to navigate campus parking which can be difficult on game days (SeatGeek, Reddit).
attractions.nearby: Tiger Stadium, LSU Museum of Natural Science, Mike the Tiger's Habitat, LSU Parade Grounds (Tripadvisor, Explore Louisiana).

attractions.name: Tiger Stadium at LSU
attractions.description: Tiger Stadium, popularly known as 'Death Valley,' is an iconic outdoor stadium on the campus of Louisiana State University and serves as the legendary home of LSU football. Renowned for its electrifying atmosphere and massive seating capacity of over 102,000, it is consistently ranked as one of the most intimidating and raucous venues for visiting teams in college sports LSU Sports, Wikipedia)
attractions.address: North Stadium Drive at Nicholson Drive, LSU Campus
attractions.hours: Campus: Open 24/7; Ticket Office: Monday–Friday 9:00 AM – 5:00 PM; Guided Tours: Select dates at 4:30 PM or 3:00 PM LSU Sports Tours, LSU Contact
attractions.days_open: Campus open year-round; Guided tours on select dates LSU Sports Tours, Explore Louisiana
attractions.admission: Guided Tours: Adults $10, Youth (Under 13) $5; Gameday Tickets: Prices vary by game, starting around $24 LSU Sports Tours, SeatGeek
highlights: Legendary night games with a capacity of 102,321 fans LSU Sports
Iconic 'Victory Hill' march led by Mike the Tiger SeatGeek
Historic 'Night the Tigers Moved the Earth' earthquake game LSU Sports
Golden Girl and LSU Tiger Marching Band performances LSU Sports
Intimidating atmosphere famously nicknamed 'Death Valley' LSU Sports
attractions.tips: Arrive 3-4 hours early to experience the legendary tailgating culture and the team's march down Victory Hill. Visit the LSU Dairy Store nearby for famous ice cream, and be sure to see Mike the Tiger at his habitat across from the stadium Explore Louisiana, SeatGeek
attractions.nearby: Mike the Tiger's Habitat, LSU Dairy Store, LSU Memorial Tower, Jack and Priscilla Andonie Museum, Magnolia Mound Plantation Visit Baton Rouge, Explore Louisiana

attractions.name: Downtown Baton Rouge (Downtown Development District)
attractions.description: Downtown Baton Rouge is the city's vibrant cultural and economic heart, situated along the Mississippi River. It serves as a walkable neighborhood home to state government offices, historic districts, and a wide array of restaurants, entertainment venues, and parks (Downtown Development District, Wikipedia).
attractions.region: East Baton Rouge Parish / Downtown (includes Spanish Town and Beauregard Town)
attractions.hours: District: Open 24/7; DDD Office: Monday - Friday 8:00 AM - 5:00 PM; Museum hours vary (typically 10:00 AM - 5:00 PM) (Downtown Development District, LSU Museum of Art)
attractions.admission: Free (General area); Museums have individual fees (e.g., LSU Museum of Art $5, Louisiana Art & Science Museum $15), with "First Free Sunday" programs for several venues (Downtown Development District)
highlights: Louisiana State Capitol (tallest in the U.S.)
Old State Capitol ("Castle on the River")
USS Kidd Veterans Museum
Mississippi River Levee Trail
Shaw Center for the Arts
Red Stick Farmers Market
attractions.tips: Visit on the first Sunday of the month for free admission to many museums. Parking is free on weekends and after 6:00 PM on weekdays in most areas (Downtown Development District, LASM).
attractions.nearby: Louisiana State University (LSU), Mid City, Baton Rouge Zoo, Mississippi River Bridge (Visit Baton Rouge)

attractions.name: Parrain's Seafood Restaurant
attractions.description: Parrain's Seafood opened in 2001 with a commitment to serving the freshest and highest quality seafood available. Located in the heart of Baton Rouge, the restaurant offers an extensive menu of traditional Cajun and Creole dishes, including daily specials crafted from the fresh catch of the day sourced exclusively from the Gulf of Mexico. (Official Website, 225 Magazine)
attractions.region: Perkins Road Overpass District / Mid-City (225 Magazine)
attractions.hours: Sun - Thu: 11:00 AM - 10:00 PM; Fri - Sat: 11:00 AM - 11:00 PM (Official Website)
attractions.days_open: Monday, Tuesday, Wednesday, Thursday, Friday, Saturday, Sunday (Official Website)
attractions.admission: Free (Official Website)
highlights: Fresh seafood sourced exclusively from the Gulf of Mexico (Official Website)
Daily specials that change based on the freshest local catch (Visit Baton Rouge)
Signature Louisiana dishes like Char-grilled Oysters, BBQ Shrimp, and Seafood Gumbo (Tripadvisor)
Located in the culturally rejuvenated Perkins Road Overpass district (225 Magazine)
Vibrant bar and casual dining atmosphere perfect for any occasion (Visit Baton Rouge)
attractions.tips: Be sure to try the bread pudding for dessert, as it is a local favorite. Check the daily specials for unique dishes not on the regular menu. If you are having trouble finding the entrance, continue to the far back of the parking lot from the main sign on Perkins Road. (Tripadvisor, Evendo)
attractions.nearby: Perkins Road Community Park, Louisiana State University (LSU) Campus, The Chimes restaurant, and the Perkins Road Overpass cultural district (Tripadvisor, Dove Creek Apartments)

attractions.name: BREC's Baton Rouge Zoo
attractions.description: BREC’s Baton Rouge Zoo is a premier family attraction that connects visitors with wildlife through immersive exhibits and conservation programs. Home to over 900 animals from around the world, the zoo focuses on education and protecting endangered species. Official Site
attractions.hours: Daily: 9:30 AM – 5:00 PM (Last entry at 4:00 PM). Official Site
attractions.days_open: Open daily except for New Year's Day, Thanksgiving, Christmas Eve, and Christmas Day. Official Site
attractions.admission: Adults/Teens: $10.00, Seniors (65+): $8.50, Ages 2-12: $7.00, Ages 1 & Under: FREE. Wednesday Afternoon Discount (2pm-4pm): $3.00 + tax. Official Site
highlights: Realm of the Tiger: Featuring Sumatran and Malayan tigers and Siamang gibbons. BREC
Twiga Oasis: An interactive giraffe-feeding experience. Visit Baton Rouge
L'aquarium de Louisiane: Showcasing local aquatic species. Official Site
Safari Playground and KidsZoo: Dedicated areas for children's play and animal interaction. Official Site
Cypress Bayou Railroad: A scenic train ride through the zoo grounds. Explore Louisiana
Conservation Success: Active participation in Species Survival Plans for animals like the Golden Lion Tamarin. Official Site
attractions.tips: Enter through the new entrance at Greenwood Community Park (13350 Highway 19) instead of the old Thomas Road entrance. Visit on Wednesday afternoons between 2 PM and 4 PM for a significant admission discount. Wear comfortable walking shoes as the zoo is spread out with natural winding paths. Official Site, Northshore Parent
attractions.nearby: Greenwood Community Park, Liberty Lagoon, Knock Knock Children's Museum, USS Kidd Veterans Museum, LSU Rural Life Museum. Tripadvisor

attractions.name: North Sherwood Forest Community Park
attractions.description: North Sherwood Forest Community Park is a premier 133-acre facility in north Baton Rouge, set on land once home to a World War II-era depot. It is designed as a family-friendly destination featuring diverse recreational amenities including a 3-acre fishing lake, a unique "Virtual Park" exergaming area, and extensive indoor and outdoor fitness facilities (BREC, Visit Baton Rouge).
attractions.hours: Park: Daily (Sunrise to Sunset); Recreation Center: Mon-Thurs 6 a.m.-8 p.m., Fri 6 a.m.-7 p.m., Sat 9 a.m.-2 p.m., Sun (Rentals only); Indoor Walking Track: Mon-Thurs 6 a.m.-8 p.m., Fri 6 a.m.-7 p.m., Sat 9 a.m.-2 p.m. (BREC, Well-Ahead Louisiana).
attractions.admission: Free for general park use, including the indoor walking track; fees apply for specific facility rentals and fitness center memberships (BREC, Well-Ahead Louisiana).
highlights: 3-acre fishing lake with two piers for bank fishing (BREC)
Innovative 'Virtual Park' exergaming facility for fitness and gaming (Visit Baton Rouge)
Free 0.15-mile indoor, air-conditioned walking track (Well-Ahead Louisiana)
Extensive playground and splash pad areas for children ages 2-12 (ELS Landscape Architecture)
Historically significant site of the former Federal Sharp Station Depot (BREC)
Multiple fitness centers and outdoor fitness stations (BREC)
attractions.tips: A valid Louisiana fishing permit is required for fishing in the lake. The indoor walking track is a great way to stay active during hot or rainy days, and the 'Virtual Park' is recommended for group rentals or birthday parties (BREC, Red Stick Life).
attractions.nearby: Baton Rouge Zoo, Mike the Tiger’s Habitat at LSU, and the Red Stick Farmers Market (BREC, Family Destinations Guide).

attractions.name: Scotlandville
attractions.description: Scotlandville is a historic community in north Baton Rouge, originally an independent rural town that became a major Black residential area during the Great Migration. It is home to Southern University and A&M College, and features the Scotlandville Parkway, a 134-acre recreation area that serves as a central green space for the community.
attractions.hours: Daily, from sunrise to sunset
attractions.admission: Free
highlights: Home to Southern University and A&M College, a historic land-grant university.
Scotlandville Parkway, a 134-acre park designated as a National Recreation Trail.
The Scotlandville Heart Trail, a 2.5-mile guided walking path featuring local art.
Southern Bluffs Art Walk, showcasing murals and art pieces along the Parkway.
Significant historical role as one of the largest majority-Black communities in Louisiana.
Baton Rouge Metropolitan Airport is located within the community boundaries.
attractions.tips: Visit the Scotlandville Parkway for outdoor activities like disc golf and basketball, and follow the Heart Trail for a scenic walk through the neighborhood. The area is best explored by car, although the parkway offers excellent biking and walking paths.
attractions.nearby: Southern University and A&M College, Baton Rouge Metropolitan Airport, Greenwood Community Park, Baton Rouge Zoo.

attractions.name: Southern University and A&M College
attractions.description: Southern University and A&M College is a public historically black land-grant university and the flagship institution of the Southern University System. Located on the scenic Scott's Bluff overlooking the Mississippi River, it is renowned for its academic programs, vibrant student life, and the world-famous "Human Jukebox" marching band (Wikipedia, Visit Baton Rouge).
attractions.hours: Main Campus Offices: Mon-Fri 8:00 AM - 5:00 PM; Museum of Art: Mon-Fri 10:30 AM - 4:30 PM; Campus Tours: Mon-Thu 10:00 AM & 2:00 PM (Southern University, Southern University)
attractions.days_open: Monday - Friday (Business/Museum); Campus grounds open daily (Southern University)
attractions.admission: Free (Visit Baton Rouge, Southern University)
highlights: The "Red Stick" sculpture, marking the spot that gave Baton Rouge its name
Scenic views of the Mississippi River from Scott's Bluff
Southern University Museum of Art (SUMA) featuring African and African American art
The legendary "Human Jukebox" marching band and Fabulous Dancing Dolls
A.W. Mumford Stadium, home of the Southern Jaguars football team
Historic campus landmarks including the Smith-Brown Memorial Union
attractions.tips: Schedule campus tours at least one week in advance for the best experience. Visit during a home football game to witness the incredible energy of the Human Jukebox and local tailgating culture. The Red Stick sculpture is a must-see for history buffs and offers great photo opportunities (Southern University, Visit Baton Rouge).
attractions.nearby: Baton Rouge Zoo, Anna T. Jordan Community Park, Mississippi River, Louisiana State Capitol (Visit Baton Rouge)

attractions.name: Atchafalaya Basin (Atchafalaya National Heritage Area)
attractions.description: The Atchafalaya Basin is the nation's largest river swamp, encompassing nearly one million acres of bottomland hardwoods, swamps, bayous, and backwater lakes. It is one of the most ecologically varied regions in the United States, serving as a critical habitat for diverse wildlife and a cornerstone of Louisiana's Cajun cultural heritage.
attractions.region: Atchafalaya National Heritage Area / St. Martin Parish
attractions.hours: 8:30 a.m. - 5:00 p.m. (Welcome Center)
attractions.days_open: Daily (Closed New Year's Day, Easter Sunday, Thanksgiving Day, Christmas Eve, and Christmas Day)
attractions.admission: Free (Atchafalaya Welcome Center and general access); specific airboat or swamp tours operated by private vendors vary in price.
highlights: Nation's largest river swamp and largest contiguous bottomland hardwood forest in North America.
Home to over 250 bird species and the largest nesting concentration of bald eagles in the south-central U.S.
Produces an average annual commercial harvest of nearly 22 million pounds of crawfish.
Contains over 100 different species of fish and aquatic life.
Recognized as the Atchafalaya National Heritage Area for its unique cultural and historical significance.
Five times more productive than any other river basin in North America.
attractions.tips: Visit the Atchafalaya Welcome Center at I-10 Exit 121 to watch the award-winning introductory film and view animated wildlife exhibits. The area is excellent for birding and photography, but visitors should bring insect repellent and prepare for humid swamp conditions. Booking a guided airboat tour is recommended for those wanting to explore the deeper interior of the swamp.
attractions.nearby: Louisiana Airborne Memorial Bridge, Sherburne Complex Wildlife Management Area, Lake Martin, Historic Downtown Lafayette, Whiskey Bay.

attractions.name: Audubon State Historic Site & Oakley House
attractions.description: Nestled within a 100-acre forest, Oakley Plantation is the historic heart of the Audubon State Historic Site where naturalist John James Audubon lived and worked in 1821. The site preserves the plantation's Federal-style house, detached kitchen, and slave cabins, offering a window into the early 19th-century Louisiana life that inspired many of Audubon's famous bird paintings. Louisiana State Parks, Explore Louisiana
attractions.hours: Daily: 9:00 AM - 5:00 PM (Closed Thanksgiving, Christmas, and New Year's Day) Louisiana State Parks
attractions.admission: General Admission (Grounds/Gardens): $6 (ages 7-61), $4 (62+), Free (6 & under); Guided House Tour (includes grounds): $10 (ages 7-61), $8 (62+), Free (6 & under) Louisiana State Parks
highlights: Guided tours of the 17-room Oakley House, a premier example of Federal-style architecture adapted for the Southern climate.
The 100-acre woodland grounds where John James Audubon created 32 of his "Birds of America" paintings.
Reconstructed detached plantation kitchen and original slave cabins highlighting the history of enslaved laborers.
Restored formal and kitchen gardens featuring period-appropriate plants and landscaping.
Extensive nature trails for birdwatching and hiking through lush magnolia and poplar forests.
attractions.tips: Wear comfortable walking shoes to explore the 100-acre grounds and nature trails. Plan for at least 2 hours to see the house, gardens, and outbuildings. Check the event calendar for living history demonstrations like blacksmithing or open-hearth cooking. Louisiana State Parks, Tripadvisor
attractions.nearby: Rosedown Plantation State Historic Site, The Myrtles Plantation, St. Francisville Historic District, Cat Island National Wildlife Refuge, West Feliciana Historical Society Museum Visit St. Francisville

attractions.name: Audubon State Historic Site (Oakley Plantation)
attractions.description: Audubon State Historic Site is the 100-acre setting of Oakley Plantation, where renowned artist-naturalist John James Audubon lived in 1821 while painting 32 of his famous 'Birds of America' series. The site features the historic Oakley House, restored formal gardens, reconstructed slave cabins, and a mature forest that continues to inspire birdwatchers and nature enthusiasts today Louisiana State Parks.
attractions.hours: Daily 9:00 a.m. – 5:00 p.m. Louisiana State Parks
attractions.days_open: Daily (Monday - Sunday), except Thanksgiving, Christmas, and New Year's Day Louisiana State Parks
attractions.admission: General Admission: $6 per person (ages 7-61), $4 seniors (62+), Free for children (6 & under); Guided House Tour (includes grounds): $10 per person (ages 7-61), $8 seniors (62+), Free for children (6 & under) Louisiana State Parks
highlights: Oakley House, a splendid example of colonial architecture and National Register of Historic Places site
Lush 100-acre forest and nature trail where Audubon painted his bird illustrations
Reconstructed detached plantation kitchen with a weaving and wash room
Original slave cabins that provide historical context on the plantation's laborers
Restored formal and kitchen gardens reflecting early Louisiana plantation life
Interpretive center and museum showcasing the site's history and Audubon's work
attractions.tips: Guided tours of the Oakley House are offered daily on the hour from 10 a.m. to 4 p.m. and are highly recommended for the full history; the site is a premier birdwatching destination, so bring binoculars to spot local species like the Mississippi Kite or Pileated Woodpecker Audubon State Historic Site Wordpress, Birding Louisiana.
attractions.nearby: Rosedown Plantation State Historic Site, The Myrtles Plantation, Port Hudson State Historic Site, Tunica Hills Wildlife Management Area, Cat Island National Wildlife Refuge Visit St. Francisville, Explore Louisiana

attractions.name: Blue Bayou Waterpark
attractions.description: Blue Bayou Waterpark is Louisiana’s favorite family-friendly destination, offering endless fun with wild rides, miles of slides, and cool summer attractions. The park features over 20 attractions, including a massive wave pool and free unlimited fountain drinks for all guests. Official Site, Wikipedia
attractions.address: 18142 Perkins Rd E, Baton Rouge, LA 70810
attractions.hours: Regular Hours: 10:00 AM – 6:00 PM daily during peak season (some days 10:00 AM – 5:00 PM). Opens May 16-17, then daily starting May 22 through Aug 9. Weekends only from Aug 10 through Labor Day. New Orleans Magazine, Official Site
attractions.days_open: Daily from late May through early August; Weekends in mid-May and from mid-August through Labor Day. New Orleans Magazine, Official Site
attractions.admission: Single Day Tickets: Starting at $29.99 (Online Advanced Purchase); Season Pass: $79.99; 2-Waterpark Season Pass (includes Gulf Islands Waterpark): $89.99; No free admission for non-swimmers. New Orleans Magazine, Official Site
highlights: Mambo: A thrilling quadruple aqualoop slide.
Hurricane Bay: A massive wave pool mimicking Gulf Coast swells.
Voodoo: A dark, enclosed tunnel slide with unpredictable twists.
Pirate's Cove: An ultimate kids' zone with mini-slides and spray features.
Free Unlimited Fountain Drinks: Included with every admission. Official Site
attractions.tips: Purchase tickets online in advance to save on gate prices. Book cabanas early as they frequently sell out on weekends. All guests must pay admission regardless of whether they plan to swim. Use a locker to secure valuables during your visit. Official Site
attractions.nearby: Laissez Versez Distillery, Bluebonnet Swamp Nature Center, Mall of Louisiana, LSU Hilltop Arboretum, Perkins Rowe. Tripadvisor

attractions.name: Bluebonnet Swamp Nature Center
attractions.description: Bluebonnet Swamp Nature Center is a 103-acre nature conservation park in Baton Rouge dedicated to conservation, education, recreation, and tourism. The facility features a variety of habitats, including cypress-tupelo swamps and beech-magnolia forests, connected by over a mile of gravel paths and boardwalks. BREC, Wikipedia
attractions.hours: Tuesday - Saturday: 9:00 a.m. - 5:00 p.m.; Sunday: 12:00 p.m. - 5:00 p.m.; Monday: Closed BREC
attractions.admission: Ages 2 and younger: Free; Ages 3-17: $2; Ages 18-64: $3; Ages 65 and older: $2.50; Full-time college students with university ID: $2.50 BREC
highlights: Over a mile of gravel paths and boardwalks through diverse swamp and forest habitats.
A 9,500-square-foot award-winning exhibit building featuring live Louisiana wildlife.
The state's largest public display of vintage waterfowl decoy carvings.
Abundant wildlife viewing including hundreds of bird species, turtles, and mammals.
Interactive educational displays and touch tables for hands-on learning.
Annual special events such as "Duck Duck Goose Day" and "Rockin\
attractions.tips: Last admission to the trails is at 4:15 p.m. daily. The center maintains a strict no-pets policy, except during designated "Dog Days" events throughout the year. BREC, KPEL News
attractions.nearby: Mall of Louisiana, LSU Rural Life Museum, Burden Museum & Gardens, Tiger Stadium, L'Auberge Casino & Hotel Expedia, Visit Baton Rouge

attractions.name: Bluebonnet Swamp Nature Center (BREC's Bluebonnet Swamp Nature Center)
attractions.description: Bluebonnet Swamp Nature Center is a 103-acre facility dedicated to conservation, education, and recreation in Baton Rouge. It features over a mile of gravel paths and boardwalks through diverse habitats like cypress-tupelo swamps and hardwood forests, complemented by a 9,500-square-foot exhibit building with live animal displays and nature exhibits (BREC, Wikipedia).
attractions.region: South Baton Rouge / East Baton Rouge Parish
attractions.hours: Tue-Sat: 9 a.m. - 5 p.m.; Sunday: noon - 5 p.m.; Closed Monday; Last trail admission: 4:15 p.m. (BREC)
attractions.admission: Ages 2 and younger: Free; Ages 3-17: $2; Ages 18-64: $3; Ages 65 and older: $2.50; Full-time college students with ID: $2.50 (BREC)
highlights: Over a mile of gravel paths and boardwalks through swamp and forest habitats (BREC)
9,500-square-foot exhibit building with live animal displays and nature exhibits (Wikipedia)
Large public display of vintage Louisiana duck decoys (Wikipedia)
Diverse wildlife viewing including birds, turtles, snakes, and seasonal otters (BREC)
Monthly bird walks and special events like 'Rockin' at the Swamp' (Wikipedia)

The facility features over a mile of gravel paths and boardwalks winding through diverse habitats including cypress-tupelo swamp, beech-magnolia, and hardwood forests. Visitors can explore a 9,500-square-foot award-winning exhibit building filled with live animal displays, natural artifacts, and a large collection of vintage waterfowl decoys. BREC, Explore Louisiana
attractions.address: 10503 North Oak Hills Parkway, Baton Rouge, LA 70810
attractions.hours: Monday: Closed; Tuesday - Saturday: 9:00 AM - 5:00 PM; Sunday: 12:00 PM - 5:00 PM (Last admission to trails at 4:15 PM). BREC, News Talk 96.5 KPEL

highlights: Award-winning 9,500-square-foot exhibit building with live animal displays and natural artifacts. BREC
Over a mile of gravel paths and elevated boardwalks through swamp and forest habitats. Visit Baton Rouge
One of the largest public displays of vintage duck decoys in Louisiana. News Talk 96.5 KPEL
Premier research station for the Louisiana Bird Observatory with regular bird banding programs. News Talk 96.5 KPEL
Diverse wildlife sightings including Barred Owls, Prothonotary Warblers, turtles, and occasional deer or otters. Birdingplaces.eu
Special seasonal events like "Rockin\
and

Bringing insect repellent and water is recommended, though water fountains and restrooms are available in the main building. News Talk 96.5 KPEL, BREC
attractions.nearby: Mall of Louisiana, Perkins Rowe (shopping and dining), LSU Rural Life Museum, Burden Museum & Gardens. Visit Baton Rouge, Hernandez Lawnscape

attractions.name: Burden Museum & Gardens
attractions.description: Burden Museum & Gardens is a 440-acre destination in Baton Rouge that offers a window into Louisiana's cultural and natural heritage. It features the LSU Rural Life Museum, the LSU AgCenter Botanic Gardens, and the historic Windrush Gardens, providing a mix of outdoor history and diverse botanical collections. LSU AgCenter
attractions.region: Baton Rouge Health District / South Baton Rouge
attractions.hours: Gates: 8:00 a.m. – 4:30 p.m. daily; Grounds: Open until 5:00 p.m. LSU Rural Life Museum
attractions.admission: Botanic Gardens and Trees & Trails: Free. LSU Rural Life Museum and Windrush Gardens: Adults (12-61) $12, Seniors (62+) $11, Children (4-11) $10, Children under 3 Free, LSU Faculty/Staff/Students and Military $10. LSU Rural Life Museum, LSU AgCenter
highlights: LSU Rural Life Museum, a top-rated outdoor museum featuring over 30 historic buildings.
Windrush Gardens, 25 acres of formal and informal gardens designed by Steele Burden.
Trees and Trails, offering five miles of serene walking paths through Burden Woods.
Steele Burden Memorial Orangerie, a unique architectural landmark for events and plant displays.
Barton Arboretum and the Black Swamp boardwalk.
Specialty gardens including the Rose Garden, Children’s Garden, and Herb Garden.
attractions.tips: Allow at least two hours to fully explore the Rural Life Museum and 30-45 minutes for Windrush Gardens. Note that photography sessions require a permit and fees, and the site is closed on major holidays including Thanksgiving and Christmas. Visit Baton Rouge, LSU Rural Life Museum
attractions.nearby: Bluebonnet Swamp Nature Center, Mall of Louisiana, Perkins Rowe. Yelp, Reddit

attractions.name: Catfish Town (at Bally's Baton Rouge)
attractions.category: Historic District, Entertainment, Landmark National Register of Historic Places, Visit Baton Rouge
attractions.description: Catfish Town is a historic warehouse district in downtown Baton Rouge, originally developed in the late 19th and early 20th centuries as a rail and river freight hub. In 1984, it was transformed into a $28 million festival marketplace and is now part of the Bally's Baton Rouge Casino & Hotel complex, featuring a massive glass-enclosed atrium that preserves the area's industrial character. Wikipedia, W.E. O'Neil Construction
attractions.address: 103 France Street, Baton Rouge, LA 70802 Bally's Baton Rouge
attractions.region: Beauregard Town / Downtown Downtown Development District
attractions.hours: Open 24/7 Bally's Baton Rouge
attractions.admission: Free Bally's Baton Rouge
highlights: Historic late 19th-century brick warehouses with signature shallow arches Beauregard Town History
A massive 50,000-square-foot glass-enclosed atrium connecting the buildings 225 Magazine
Original Catfish Town marketplace plaque and historic railroad tracks on display Talk 107.3
Prime riverfront location along the Mississippi River levee Explore Louisiana
Contributing element to the National Register-listed Beauregard Town Historic District National Park Service
attractions.tips: Visit the Bally's atrium to see the original Catfish Town plaque and repurposed railroad artifacts. The area is best explored as part of a walking tour of the Beauregard Town historic district, especially during the day when the architectural details of the brick warehouses are most visible. 225 Magazine, Taste of the South
attractions.nearby: USS Kidd Veterans Museum, Louisiana Art & Science Museum, Old State Capitol, Raising Cane's River Center Visit Baton Rouge

attractions.name: Celebration Station

The park features meticulously designed miniature golf courses, high-speed go-karts, batting cages, and a large arcade filled with the latest video and redemption games (Official Website, Tripadvisor).

attractions.name: Centenary State Historic Site
attractions.description: Centenary State Historic Site preserves the remains of the oldest college in Louisiana, originally opened as the College of Louisiana in 1826 and later becoming Centenary College. The site features the restored 1837 West Wing dormitory and a Victorian-style professor's house, interpreting the history of education and the campus's role as a Confederate hospital and Union headquarters during the Civil War. Louisiana State Parks, Explore Louisiana
attractions.region: Baton Rouge Area / East Feliciana Parish
attractions.hours: Thursday: 9 a.m. - 5 p.m., Friday: 9 a.m. - 5 p.m., Saturday: 9 a.m. - 5 p.m., Sunday - Wednesday: Closed. Louisiana State Parks

highlights: Restored 1837 West Wing dormitory building
Late Victorian-style professor's house with period furnishings
Jackson Confederate Cemetery containing unmarked graves of soldiers

Site of the 1863 Battle of Jackson skirmish during the Civil War

attractions.tips: Buildings are typically open for special programming or by appointment, so call ahead to schedule a guided tour of the structures. The site is a great spot for birding and nature viewing in the rolling hills north of Baton Rouge. Louisiana State Parks, Explore Louisiana

attractions.name: Destrehan Plantation
attractions.description: Established in 1787, Destrehan Plantation is the oldest documented plantation home in the lower Mississippi Valley. It operates as a living history museum where costumed interpreters bring the past to life, detailing the history of the families and enslaved people who lived and worked on the property (Official Website).

attractions.hours: 9:00 am to 5:30 pm daily; Tours run every 30 minutes from 9:30 am to 4:00 pm (Official Website)
attractions.days_open: Daily except major holidays (New Year’s Day, Mardi Gras, Easter, Thanksgiving, Christmas Eve, and Christmas Day) (Official Website)

highlights: Oldest documented plantation home in the Lower Mississippi Valley (Tour Louisiana)
The Miller-Haydel Museum, featuring the 1811 Slave Revolt Exhibit (Official Website)

Expansive grounds featuring live oak trees over 200 years old (Tripadvisor)
Daily folk-life demonstrations including weaving, open-hearth cooking, and other period crafts (Official Website)
attractions.tips: Destrehan is the closest plantation to New Orleans and the only one easily accessible by rideshare or taxi. Photography is welcome on the grounds but prohibited during guided tours. Visitors should allow at least 1.5 to 2 hours to explore the house, outbuildings, and exhibits (Tripadvisor, Official Website).

attractions.description: Farr Park Equestrian Center and RV Campground is one of the largest and most versatile equestrian facilities in the region, featuring 297 acres of space for horse enthusiasts and campers alike. Located along historic River Road, it offers top-tier amenities including indoor and outdoor arenas, extensive riding trails, and a full-service RV campground. It is a popular destination for riding lessons, guided trail rides, and competitive equestrian events in the Baton Rouge area. BREC

attractions.name: Garden District Baton Rouge
attractions.description: The Garden District is a historic residential neighborhood in Baton Rouge's Mid-City area, known for its well-preserved early 20th-century architecture and majestic live oak trees. It consists of three contiguous historic districts—Roseland Terrace, Drehr Place, and Kleinert Terrace—and serves as one of the city's most successful walkable, urban communities.
attractions.hours: Open 24/7 (Public Streets)
attractions.admission: Free (Public Neighborhood)
highlights: Three National Register Historic Districts: Roseland Terrace, Drehr Place, and Kleinert Terrace
Stately collection of century-old Live Oak trees lining the medians and streets
The Reiley-Reeves House, the city's last remaining Queen Anne Revival home with a turret
Walkable access to City-Brooks Park, Baton Rouge Gallery, and local dog parks
Annual community events including the Wearin' of the Green St. Patrick's Day Parade
Unique 'Little Free Libraries' and a vibrant front-porch culture
attractions.tips: The neighborhood is best explored on foot or by bicycle to appreciate the historic architecture and oak-lined medians. Visitors should be mindful that most homes are private residences, and parking can be limited during major events like the St. Patrick's Day parade. Walking paths near the LSU lakes provide scenic views just blocks away.
attractions.nearby: City-Brooks Park, Baton Rouge Gallery, LSU Lakes, Louisiana State University Campus, Raising Cane's Dog Park

It also features unique historical elements, such as trails that interpret the site's history as a WWII ammunitions depot BREC.org, Sasaki Visit Baton Rouge

It serves as a remote-control astronomical observatory dedicated to education and recreation, housing a 20-inch reflecting telescope used to discover dozens of asteroids and an accessible 16-inch telescope (Official Website, Explore Louisiana).

attractions.name: John and Amelia Sharp Kleinpeter House (Willow Grove)
attractions.description: The John and Amelia Sharp Kleinpeter House, also known as Willow Grove, is a meticulously restored circa-1820 Creole-style plantation home located in Baton Rouge. It serves as the historic centerpiece of The Settlement at Willow Grove, a traditional neighborhood development built on the family’s ancestral farmland to preserve its agricultural legacy. The landmark is listed on the National Register of Historic Places and features rare architectural details such as authentic bousillage construction. (Source, Source)

attractions.region: The Settlement at Willow Grove / Southeast Baton Rouge

highlights: Listed on the National Register of Historic Places (2015).
Exemplifies the rare Creole architectural style with Federal-era detailing.
Restored using traditional bousillage (mud and Spanish moss) wall construction.
Features an on-site historic family cemetery and a 19th-century brick-lined well.
Located in Baton Rouge’s first Traditional Neighborhood Development (TND).
Surrounded by a preserved grove of centuries-old live oak trees.
attractions.tips: Take a self-guided walking tour through the Village at Willow Grove to enjoy its local shops and dining. Visit during daylight hours to best appreciate the historic architecture and the surrounding landscaped parks and trails. (Source)

attractions.name: Lea's Lunchroom, Inc.
attractions.description: Lea's Lunchroom is a historic Louisiana eatery established in 1928, famous for its homemade pies and traditional Southern fare. It has served generations of locals and travelers as a premier roadside destination and helped earn its hometown the title of "Pie Capital of Louisiana." The restaurant is particularly renowned for its high-meringue pies and signature baked ham sandwiches (Lea's Lunchroom).
attractions.hours: Monday: Closed; Tuesday: 7:00 AM – 3:00 PM; Wednesday: 7:00 AM – 3:00 PM; Thursday: 7:00 AM – 3:00 PM; Friday: 7:00 AM – 3:00 PM; Saturday: 7:00 AM – 3:00 PM; Sunday: 7:00 AM – 3:00 PM (Lea's Lunchroom)
attractions.admission: Free (Lea's Lunchroom)
highlights: World-famous homemade pies with high meringue, with nearly 65,000 baked annually (Only In Your State).
Established in 1928 by Lea Johnson, making it a historic landmark and long-standing Louisiana tradition (Lea's Lunchroom).
Known for its signature baked ham, served in sandwiches or as a daily plate lunch special (Lea's Lunchroom).
Located in Lecompte, which was officially designated the "Pie Capital of Louisiana" by the state legislature in 2001 (Only In Your State).
A quintessential roadside stop for travelers moving between northern and southern Louisiana (Explore Louisiana).
attractions.tips: Be mindful that the lunch line closes strictly at 2:30 PM, which is 30 minutes before the restaurant's closing time (Lea's Lunchroom). Breakfast is only available until 10:30 AM before the menu switches to lunch (Lea's Lunchroom). It is highly recommended to call ahead to reserve whole pies, especially during holiday seasons, as they often sell out (Lea's Lunchroom).

attractions.description: Locust Grove State Historic Site commemorates a small family cemetery that is the last remaining part of the historic Locust Grove Plantation. Visitors can explore the graves of notable figures such as Sarah Knox Taylor, the daughter of President Zachary Taylor and wife of Jefferson Davis, and War of 1812 hero Major General Eleazar Ripley (Louisiana State Parks, Visit St. Francisville).

Grave of Major General Eleazar Wheelock Ripley, a hero of the War of 1812 (Explore Louisiana)

Educational programs and special events like grave-rubbing demonstrations (Louisiana State Parks)

attractions.name: Louisiana State Capitol (Wikipedia)
attractions.description: The Louisiana State Capitol is the seat of government for the state of Louisiana and is the tallest state capitol in the United States, standing at 450 feet with 34 stories. Completed in 1931, the Art Deco skyscraper serves as a monument to Governor Huey Long, who is buried on the grounds, and houses the Louisiana Legislature and the Governor's office (Wikipedia, Visit Baton Rouge).
attractions.address: 900 North Third Street, Baton Rouge, LA 70802 (Louisiana Welcome Centers)
attractions.hours: Daily: 8:00 AM – 4:30 PM (Observation Tower closes at 4:00 PM). Closed on New Year's Day, Easter Sunday, Thanksgiving Day, Christmas Eve, and Christmas Day (Louisiana Welcome Centers).
attractions.admission: Free (Louisiana Welcome Centers, US Civil Rights Trail)
highlights: Tallest state capitol building in the United States at 450 feet (Wikipedia)
Memorial Hall features a map of Louisiana in marble and is the site of Governor Huey Long's assassination (Wikipedia)
27th-floor Observation Deck offering panoramic views of Baton Rouge and the Mississippi River (Louisiana Welcome Centers)
Extensive 27-acre Capitol Gardens including the burial site of Huey Long (Wikipedia)
Intricate Art Deco architecture and relief sculptures depicting Louisiana history (Wikipedia)
attractions.tips: As of early 2026, the observation deck has undergone intermittent renovations; visitors should call ahead to verify current access. Guided tours are available by contacting staff, and the ground floor cafeteria is open to the public during weekday mornings (Louisiana Welcome Centers, LADDC).
attractions.nearby: Capitol Park Museum, Louisiana's Old State Capitol, Governor's Mansion, Arsenal Park, Mississippi River, Shaw Center for the Arts (Expedia, Louisiana Welcome Centers)

attractions.name: Louisiana State University and Agricultural and Mechanical College (LSU)
attractions.description: Louisiana State University (LSU) is a public land-grant research university and the flagship institution of the state. Its picturesque 2,000-acre campus is known for its Italian Renaissance-style architecture, ancient oak trees, and the University Lakes. Visitors can explore world-class museums, see the live tiger mascot, and experience the electric atmosphere of Tiger Stadium.

highlights: Mike the Tiger’s Habitat: Home to the university’s live Bengal-Siberian tiger mascot.
Tiger Stadium: An iconic 102,000-seat stadium known as "Death Valley."
LSU Rural Life Museum: A sprawling outdoor museum dedicated to Louisiana's cultural history.
LSU Museum of Natural Science: Features extensive collections of zoological and archaeological artifacts.
Memorial Tower: A campus landmark honoring Louisianians who died in World War I.
University Lakes: Scenic areas surrounding the campus perfect for walking and recreation.
attractions.tips: Start your visit at the Welcome Center (temporarily in Union Square Garage) for maps and parking passes. To see Mike the Tiger active, visit during the cooler morning hours. attractions.name: LSU Rural Life Museum
attractions.description: The LSU Rural Life Museum is a 25-acre open-air museum dedicated to the preservation of 18th and 19th-century Louisiana material culture. It features the state's largest collection of vernacular architecture, including over 30 historic buildings that illustrate the daily lives of the working classes and enslaved persons of the era (LSU Rural Life Museum).
attractions.hours: Daily 8:00 AM - 5:00 PM (Visitor Center and structures close at 4:30 PM). Closed New Year's Day, Easter Sunday, Thanksgiving, Christmas Eve, and Christmas Day (LSU Rural Life Museum).

Windrush Gardens, a 25-acre landscaped sanctuary with majestic live oaks (LSU Rural Life Museum)
Extensive collection of 19th-century artifacts, tools, and horse-drawn carriages (Wikipedia)

Annual cultural events such as Harvest Days and a Rural Life Christmas (LSU Rural Life Museum)
attractions.tips: Allow at least 2 hours for the museum and 45 minutes for the gardens; wear comfortable walking shoes and bring insect repellent, as mosquitoes can be prevalent (LSU Rural Life Museum, Tripadvisor).

attractions.name: Magnolia Mound: Museum + Historic Site
attractions.description: Magnolia Mound is a rare survivor of vernacular architecture influenced by early French and West Indian settlers, featuring a historic plantation house originally built in 1791. The 16-acre site serves as a museum accredited by the American Alliance of Museums, illustrating the French Creole lifestyle through its collection of furnishings and documented settings. (BREC, Wikipedia)

attractions.name: Manship Theatre at Shaw Center for the Arts

It serves as a hub for music, theater, dance, and film, offering an intimate 325-seat setting with exceptional acoustics. The theater is also dedicated to community enrichment through its diverse educational programs and rotating art exhibitions in its mezzanine gallery (Manship Theatre, 225 Magazine).

It features a unique sandy beach and a handicapped-accessible pier, making it a popular destination for sunbathing, reading, and wildlife viewing. Visitors frequently enjoy the park for outdoor activities such as kayaking, paddleboarding, fishing, and exercising at the on-site fitness stations. BREC Official Website, Visit Baton Rouge

attractions.name: North Boulevard Town Square
attractions.description: North Boulevard Town Square is a spectacular greenspace development that serves as a lively downtown center where cultural and civic attractions converge. The space features over an acre of open green space, innovative water features, and high-tech amenities such as a media beacon to entertain and cool visitors. It transformed a previously disjointed area into a premier social gathering place that hosts weekly community events, concerts, and festivals. (Downtown Development District, Dana Brown and Associates)

attractions.name: Nottoway Plantation & Resort (Wikipedia)
attractions.description: Nottoway Plantation is a historic landmark and resort located near White Castle, Louisiana, known for being the site of the largest antebellum mansion in the Southern United States. Although the main Greek Revival and Italianate-style mansion was largely destroyed by a fire in May 2025, the property remains open for guided tours of its surviving structures and grounds. Visitors can still enjoy dining at Randolph's Restaurant and explore the site's complex history along the Mississippi River. (ABC News, Wikipedia)
attractions.hours: Guided Tours: Daily 10:00 AM – 4:00 PM; Randolph's Restaurant: Thursday – Saturday: Lunch 11:00 AM – 2:00 PM, Dinner 5:00 PM – 9:00 PM; Sunday Brunch: 10:00 AM – 2:00 PM. (Whichmuseum, Yahoo)
attractions.admission: Guided Grounds Tours: $15.00 per person; Children (0-5): Free (Unfiltered with Kiran, Whichmuseum)
highlights: Surviving original structures like the garçonnière and Overseer's Cottage (Wikipedia)
The Randolph family cemetery, which was relocated back to the property in 2003 (Wikipedia)
Randolph's Restaurant, featuring eclectic Louisiana favorites (Yahoo)
Scenic location along the Mississippi River Road with close proximity to the river levee (Tripadvisor)

Reconstructed stables repurposed as a grand ballroom for events (Wikipedia)
attractions.tips: Visitors are encouraged to call ahead for tour availability and restaurant reservations, as restoration efforts following the 2025 fire may impact schedules. Comfortable walking shoes are recommended for the outdoor grounds tours. (Unfiltered with Kiran)

attractions.name: Old South Baton Rouge (OSBR)
attractions.description: Old South Baton Rouge (OSBR) is a historic three-square-mile district situated between downtown Baton Rouge and Louisiana State University. Once a thriving, self-sustained African American community during the segregation era, it is currently the focus of a major revitalization initiative to preserve its rich cultural heritage, including its unique 'Baton Rouge Bungalows' and civil rights landmarks. Wikipedia, UL Press
attractions.address: Neighborhood roughly bound by Interstate 10 (north and east), West Roosevelt Street (south), and Nicholson Drive (west)

Dr. Leo S. Butler House: The historic home and office of the 'Dean of Black Physicians,' a key community hospital site during Jim Crow. BRLA.gov

Visit the historic Lincoln Theater and Dr. Leo Butler house for a deep dive into the area's civil rights history. Visit Baton Rouge, 225 Magazine

The shop features a charming, old-school atmosphere adorned with Ragin' Cajuns memorabilia and is frequently recognized as one of the best budget dining spots in the country (Tripadvisor).

attractions.name: Port Hudson State Historic Site
attractions.description: Port Hudson State Historic Site preserves the site of the longest siege in American military history, which occurred during the Civil War from May 23 to July 9, 1863. The 643-acre site features well-preserved earthworks, artillery displays, and a museum that details the significance of the battle and the first use of African-American troops in combat under African-American leadership. Wikipedia, Louisiana State Parks

First combat use of African-American troops under African-American field leadership

attractions.name: San Francisco Plantation House
attractions.description: San Francisco Plantation is an opulent 1856 mansion in Garyville, Louisiana, famous for its unique "Steamboat Gothic" architecture and vibrant, hand-painted interiors. Though its public museum operations were discontinued in 2022 by Marathon Petroleum, it remains a National Historic Landmark and a prominent site along the Mississippi River's historic River Road.

Elaborate hand-painted ceilings, walls, and faux-wood graining throughout the interior.
Designated as a National Historic Landmark in 1974.
Historic grounds featuring a 19th-century schoolhouse and slave quarters.
Dramatic setting on the Mississippi River, surrounded by the Garyville Refinery.
attractions.tips: As of 2026, the house is closed to the public and its future remains uncertain, though the exterior can still be viewed from Highway 44 or the Mississippi River levee. Historical visitors recommended allowing at least two hours to explore the grounds and museum exhibits.

attractions.name: USS KIDD Veterans Memorial & Museum
attractions.description: The USS KIDD Veterans Museum features the world's only Fletcher-class destroyer preserved in its 1945 World War II configuration, nicknamed the "Pirate of the Pacific." The complex serves as a memorial to Louisiana veterans and houses an extensive collection of naval artifacts, aircraft, and one of the largest ship model collections in the South. Visitors can explore the ship's decks and compartments to experience authentic life at sea during WWII. (USS KIDD, Visit Baton Rouge)

Originally opened in 1937 as a movie theater, it was transformed in 1990 into a premier concert hall that hosts a wide range of national touring artists and local talent. As of early 2026, the venue has temporarily closed its doors for extensive renovations to preserve its historic charm while updating its facilities (Visit Baton Rouge, WBRZ News).
